name: Begonia chlorosticta
aboutText: Begonia chlorosticta is a rare Bornean rainforest understory species originating from Sarawak, Borneo. It thrives in deep shade on moist, well-drained, humus-rich substrate among leaf litter. High humidity and stable warmth are essential, reflecting its native tropical forest environment. This rhizomatous begonia features striking dark foliage patterned with luminous lime-green spots. Its leaves are asymmetrical, ovate to heart-ovate, and subtly bullate, supported by reddish petioles with deep red undersides. Growth is from a creeping rhizome at the substrate surface, forming compact clumps on the forest floor.

fieldNotes.title: Pale-Spotted Borneo
fieldNotes.author: Aroid Aaron
fieldNotes.content: Begonia chlorosticta from Sarawak — the pale-dotted Bornean begonia with scattered cream-to-pale spots on dark ground — catalogued here in the 'other genera' collection. At middle tier pricing. The spotting pattern is subtler than Begonia maculata's bold polka dots, and the plant itself more demanding in terms of humidity. Worth growing in a humid cabinet where the leaf texture and spotting read properly. The kind of plant that reveals its quality gradually rather than at first glance.
